ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-23-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Roddick for and with Nancy Gonzalez. Laura Velarde-Superville in Propria Persona. Alfredo Velarde in Propria Persona.

The Parties explained circumstances and presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. Roddick's fees. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Continued To Aug-20-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Roddick for Nancy Gonzalez (Administrator). Alfredo Velarde in Propria Persona. Laura Velarde-Superville in Propria Persona.

Alfredo Velarde requested a continuance to give him time to file a declaration in objection to the petition. Mr. Roddick is not opposed to this request. The Petition re accounting was continued to August 20, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The declaration is to be filed by August 13, 2014.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-16-2014 Continued To Jul-23-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Roddick for and with Nancy Gonzalez and Alfredo Velarde. Laura Velarde-Superville in Propria Persona.

The Court expressed concern about the amount of the attorney's fees expended and the amount of shares to each of the Beneficiaries. The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the attorney fees and who should be responsible to pay said fees. Mr. Velarde raised the issue fees having already been paid to Mr. Roddick. Mr. Roddick confirmed that $97,000.00 has already been and stated that it was an agreement between the Parties and not the estate. The Petition for Final Distribution was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Continued To Mar-03-11 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hal Chase for Nancy Gonzalez. David Friedeberg for Laura Vellarde (Respondent).

Mr. Chase stated that the issue of the personal property was mostly resolved, that there was the issue of ownership of the car and that they discussed setting a hearing date on the validity of the deed. In addition, Mr. Chase stated that he will present a deal to Mr. Friedeberg within a week or two and allow Mr. Friedeberg to counter offer, and see if they will consider if mediation will work. The Hearing on the Petition to Quiet Title was set for March 3, 2011 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Administration of the Estate was also continued to March 3, 2011 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Oct-20-2010 Continued To Nov-17-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hal Chase for and with Nancy Gonzalez. David Friedenberg for Laura Velarde (Respondent). Afredo Velarde also present.

Counsel stated that the personal property was in the process of being liquidated, that there was still an issue with the Decedent's car, that they were still in the process of discovery and discussed whether they should treat the issue/matter regarding interpertation of the deeds as a summary adjudication motion, set the matter for trial/evidentiary hearing or participate in mediation. The matter was continued to November 17, 2010 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery, to see if the Parties are willing to participate in mediation and for trial setting.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Sep-01-2010 Continued To Oct-05-10 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hal Chase for and with Nancy Gonzalez. David Freideberg for and with Laura Velarde (Respondent). Alfredo Velarde (Son) also present.

Counsel presented oral argument with regard to the three bank accounts, whether they were joint tenancy acounts, documents not being turned over by Mr. Friedeberg, liquidating the property, the taxes, the grant deeds, the need of an inventory, the vehicle, etc. The Court stated that it wanted the Parties to focus on what information is needed to move the matter along/forward, continued the matter for 30 days for all three Children to meet at the house and make a list of all items they are interested in, for inventory of the property, for exchange of information/documents, and for status of discovery.
